identity.name: Mother Rampion
identity.kind: witch, retired — keeps the walled garden now
identity.story: Grew the finest rampion in three tales and priced it harder than any greens are worth — the bargain that put a girl up a tower for want of a salad. Keeps a walled plot behind the cottage now, gate left open for anyone who only wants to ask this time. Never harvests the one row nearest the wall; lets it go to seed every year and has never said why, though the whole lane has a guess.

relationships.kind: grew the rampion that started the whole bargain, and leaves the tower windowbox be every high sun without a word — the one thing between them neither has finished saying
matches her turnip count row for row at the garden fence, and privately thinks her own rampion's better, which she'll never say

behavior.label: Mother Rampion's plot, kept open at the gate

child.label: walking the wall's edge before the dew's off it

children.label: training the one row of rampion she still lets go to seed
carrying a fresh cutting up to the lane
leaving the cutting on the tower princess's step, same as every lamplighting, still no word for what it's meant to say

child.label: banking the coals under a plot that keeps no secrets anymore
